frc494: Single fixed shooter, moderate throughput. Trench capable. Probably very easily defended against as they stop and shoot in open field. They have somewhat slow driving that isn't continuous. Hopper looks like it holds 25-30 fuel, and they get about 1.5 hoppers worth of fuel per active period. They have a single pass auto where they score half of their hopper's worth of fuel.
frc503: Wide drum shooter with probable performance improvement. They have a slightly smaller hopper which holds ~20-25 fuel. Fast intake, fast shooting, but not the most focused driving. Probably easier to defend, but better than most. At Troy #2 they had a 2 pass auton, but the second pass lacked much scoring. Very good passing, almost the best attribute of this robot.
frc573: Fixed two flywheel launcher. Very small hopper (10-15 fuel) that pauses in the middle to re-index. Relatively slow driving, scoring, etc. Slow intaking, and even slower launching, but is accurate. Second highest scoring match they have shows them missing almost all shots in their auto, so be cautious. Passing seems limited, but they can do it. Slow driving could make for a bad defensive pick.
frc858: Fixed three flywheel dumper. Does a single pass in auton and scores one hopper. Hopper is smaller, <15 fuel. Driving is very slow and there are lots of pauses. Looks to frequently shoot from near the corner of the hub for every teleop cycle leaving them vulnerable to defense. They appear to shuttle, but not pass. Intake easily jams on too much fuel. Appears that they have to go under the trench and they always try to go to the one nearest to their driver station.
frc1188: Turreted launcher with a relatively large hopper (~25-30 fuel). Auton clears almost a full hopper. Their launcher has good throughput with what looks like about 10bps. It does appear that they have to stop to shoot. Decent driving speed when moving, but a little jerky on the controls (lot's of stop starts). No real passing, but a lot of shuttling using their bumpers. Kind of bad intaking/navigating mid field.
frc1701: Turreted shooter. Tall bot (bump only) with big hopper (~50 fuel). Really good throughput with what looks like around 15bps. Really accurate shots, even while moving. Good passing throughput with high accuracy as well. Driving is on the faster side, but intaking can be a little slower than some. Single pass auto over the bump that usually hits 30ish fuel but has potential for more.
frc1918: Drum shooter with large hopper. Trench capable, but has a expandable hopper that can hold 50+ fuel. Two pass auto over the bump but seems to struggle scoring second pass. They need to be right next to the hub to make their shots, and sometimes they have less than good accuracy. Fast intaking in neutral zone, but struggles to easily pass. Similar to scoring they need to be at the bump to pass into the alliance zone. Driving is purposeful but there is lot's of poential to disrupt their scoring since they score in the open near the corner of the hub.
frc2224: Single fixed shooter with next to no hopper. No auto, just sits. Mainly played defense it their highest scoring matches at Troy #2. Picked as a defense only bot. Can kind of shuttle fuel using bumpers, but driving is somewhat sporadic. Defense that they played didn't appear to heavily effect the opposing alliance, and much contact was almost accidental. Stuggled to cause opponents to miss scoring.
frc2619: Single turreted robot with a small hopper. Has a level one climb in auton. Relatively slow scoring at <10bps. Intaking also seems to be relatively slow. Driving is purposeful, but there are moments where it is sporadic. No passing, but they shuttle a lot with bumpers. They seem to really like shuttling to the human player at the depot.
frc3175: Tall drum shooting robot with a big hopper (Bump only). They have a depot auto with decent accuracy. Regularly shoots from in front of the tower. Driving is purposeful but jerky. Throughput is only aobut 10bps. Intaking seems slow and they opt not to pass but instead shuttle using bumpers.
frc3536: Twin turret shooter with larger hopper (trench capable). Hopper holds rougly 30-40 fuel. Has a two pass auto, but struggles to empty the second hopper load. Fast intaking from neutral zone, but stuggles to pass and instead tries to shuttle with intake. Scoring is slower at <10bps. Frequently stops to shoot into the hub. Driving is purposeful but not the smoothest.
frc3538: Tall single turret (bump only) with relatively large hopper. High scoring throughput at around >15bps. Single pass auto that also grabs from depot. Super high accuracy. Very good shooting while moving and can effectively pass as well. Extremely purposeful driving, with only rare stoppage. Very fast intaking as well. Also frequently shuttles game pieces with bumpers.
frc3539: Single turret shooter with large hopper (trench capable). Single pass auto with about 10bps or less throughput. Decent shooting while moving and purposeful driving. Hopper holds around 30-40 fuel and they get close to 1.5 hopper worth scored per active period. Passing also looks relatively good and they can do so at their max output.
